transcript: of the aquarium with the light source and cool the other end with the ice cubes. This creates areas of high and low pressure. Place something on the aquarium that shields it from outside air. Then introduce some smoke so we can see the air moving. Kids, don't do this without parental permission and assistance. I've seen it done with a burning paper straw pinched at the end opposite the flame. Plastic straws don't work kids! With the smoke in the aquarium we can see that the wind moves from the areas of high pressure to the areas of low pressure or the cool air moves to where the warm air is. Now think of the earth. The equator is hotter than the poles. The tilt of the earth in relation to the sun changes as it orbits. The rotation of the earth and the friction of the air itself all affect the movement and creation of wind.
